assertions.text: V1 was traveling westbound in lane two of a five-lane undivided roadway (two westbound lanes, center turn lane, two eastbound lanes).
V1 traversed the lane lines and entered lane one (the adjacent westbound lane) before departing the left side of the roadway.
V1 departed the left side of the roadway and that departure initiated a clockwise yaw (rotation) of the vehicle.
V1's left side impacted an overhead traffic signal pole.
The vehicle impacted the overhead traffic signal pole (first impact) and then the front later impacted a concrete electric box (a distinct second impact).
After the left-side impact with the pole, the vehicle rotated rapidly clockwise around the pole and then split into two pieces.
The driver was ejected from V1 during the event.
The vehicle split into two pieces (a rear box and a front section) as a result of the collision with the pole.
The rear box section came to rest in the center of the roadway.
The front section continued westerly after separating, then impacted a concrete electric box with its right side plane before coming to rest facing easterly.
The front section impacted the concrete electric box after separating from the rear and while continuing westerly, then came to rest facing east.
The collision involved severe structural damage (vehicle split into two pieces) and ejection of the driver.

verdicts.evidence: The claim about lane position and roadway geometry cannot be decided from vehicle telemetry alone; speed data shows [first_value 156.2 km/h at t=-5.0 s](T3) and [last_value 122.4 km/h at t=0.0 s](T3) but lane count or direction (westbound) is not present in sensors, so this is unverifiable.

Lane traversal and crossing of lane lines are not observable in the provided channels; steering shows a peak raw value of [ -98.7 deg at t=-0.9 s](T3) and steering peak deviation [91.5 deg](T3) which indicates large steering input but does not confirm lane-to-lane movement or departure, so unverifiable.

Telemetry records steering peaks ([peak_raw_value -98.7 deg at t=-0.9 s](T3)) and crash pulse lateral delta-V of [4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T1) but device-frame rotation direction (clockwise) is not documented; a change in yaw cannot be signed from available data, therefore unverifiable.

Impact side (left side impact) and object identity (overhead traffic signal pole) are not directly recorded; crash pulse shows longitudinal delta-V [7.3 km/h final value](T3) and lateral delta-V [4.1 km/h final value](T3) which confirm an impact but not that the left side struck a signal pole, so unverifiable.

Telemetry shows a crash pulse with a longitudinal cumulative delta-V reaching [7.3 km/h at t=0.24 s](T3) and a lateral cumulative delta-V reaching [4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T1), and detect_events reported two distinct impact delta-v detections for long and lat channels (T2) consistent with a primary impact followed by a secondary impact; thus the assertion of a pole impact followed by a distinct later front impact is supported by the presence of multiple delta-V components and timings (primary/secondary) in the crash pulse (T2).

Rapid rotation is plausible from the lateral delta-V [4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T1) and steering activity ([peak_raw_value -98.7 deg at t=-0.9 s](T3)), but telemetry does not document the vehicle wrapping around a pole or splitting; structural breakup is not a sensor channel, so the sequence claiming rotation around the pole and splitting into pieces is unverifiable.
EDR summary shows driver belt code [1 meaning belted perhaps?](T1) and there is no occupant ejection channel in the telemetry; ejection cannot be determined from available sensors, so unverifiable.

Vehicle structural separation (split into rear box and front section) is not directly measured by the vehicle sensors; while large crash pulse ([7.3 km/h](T3)) indicates significant impact, splitting into two pieces cannot be inferred from telemetry alone, so unverifiable.
Rest positions (rear box in center of roadway) are not recorded by vehicle sensors; GPS position time-series is not provided, so unverifiable.

Direction of motion after separation, which side impacted the concrete box, and final facing direction are not in the telemetry; available data show crash pulse components ([7.3 km/h longitudinal](T3), [4.3 km/h lateral](T1)) but do not identify right-side impact or final orientation, so unverifiable.

The timing of impacts relative to separation is not directly observable as separate physical objects in telemetry; while secondary delta-V components exist ([delta_v_lat 4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T1)), linking that to the front section's chronology and final facing direction is not supported by the sensors, so unverifiable.

Severity: telemetry records a cumulative longitudinal delta-V of [7.3 km/h final value](T3) and lateral delta-V of [4.1 km/h final value](T3) and EDR summary lists max long delta-V [10.85 km/h](T1) (note different aggregation), which indicate a measurable crash pulse but do not directly confirm structural splitting or ejection; determining 'severe structural damage' from sensors alone is therefore unverifiable.

result.rationale: Telemetry (EDR) shows a clear crash pulse with longitudinal delta-V up to [7.3 km/h](T3) and lateral delta-V up to [4.3 km/h](T1), steering extremes ([peak_raw_value -98.7 deg](T3)), a pre-crash speed drop from [157.8 km/h to 122.4 km/h](T2)/(T3), and a brake application at [t=-0.5 s](T2); however sensors do not record lane positions, object identities, occupant ejection, or structural breakup, so many narrative specifics are unverifiable.

uncited_quantitative_claims.context: l cumulative delta-V reaching  , and detect_events reported two distinct impact delta-v detections for long and lat channels (T2) consisten

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
